data.narrative:
Saltfoss Energy is a Series A–stage nuclear energy company founded by Troels Schonfeldt, having raised $40M+ to date. The valuation universe includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors, including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy, though the narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier proved insufficient for robust median-based triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, using the $40M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the next funding stage. This yields an implied valuation of $80M. The Peer-Median method was not applied due to insufficient datapoints: while four same-sector peers exist within the broader Nuclear Energy universe, none met minimum thresholds (≥3 comps) within the filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier. A sector-wide median would risk material distortion given the heterogeneity of maturity and business models across the nuclear landscape.

The triangulation analysis supports a median valuation of $80M, with a range of $68M (low) to $92M (high) reflecting inherent uncertainty around stage-transition multiples in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector. Key uncertainties include the applicable step-up multiplier in nuclear energy specifically and the lack of granular comparable transaction data at this stage and capitalization level. The analysis rests on a single method and should be updated as additional Series A or Series B nuclear transactions become observable.
